behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is the handoff between CSV parsing (row index), schema validation, and DLQ payload construction. A unit test of validation logic alone would miss row-number propagation bugs. No OS/network dependency is required if DLQ is exercised via an in-process adapter.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline over a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields using real parser+validator+DLQ writer modules wired together; assert each rejected record is in DLQ with the exact original CSV row number.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation/parsing logic in one boundary. The failure is in date parsing/normalization rules, not module wiring or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests against the date-normalization function with ISO 8601, US, and European inputs (including ambiguous and invalid cases), asserting canonical output or explicit rejection.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on runtime memory pressure, timeout enforcement, and process-level performance characteristics. Mocked components can hide buffering/OOM regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process on a generated 500k-row CSV with production-like settings, measure wall-clock completion before timeout, and assert peak memory stays below limit (no OOM/kill).

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real HTTP error handling and retry/backoff timing behavior at the S3 client boundary. Pure mocks can miss SDK/client middleware wiring and status-code handling mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an HTTP S3-compatible endpoint/stub that returns 503 for initial attempts then succeeds; assert retries occur with increasing delays and batch eventually continues successfully.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on durable state across process restart plus real DB write semantics (transactions/checkpointing/idempotency). This cross-process persistence boundary is beyond Integration-only in-memory tests.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline against a real PostgreSQL instance, interrupt mid-batch after partial commits, restart with same input, then assert final table has exactly one copy per logical row and no duplicates.
